title: Add labor rates & parts to jobs, signature blob storage on login, and job photo capture

requirements.text: Extend the backend Job record to include a laborLineItems field: a list of records each containing laborRateId (Nat), rateType (variant { #hourly; #flat }), hours (optional Float), amount (Nat, in cents), and description (Text). Add addLaborLineItem and removeLaborLineItem endpoints on jobs restricted to #authorized. The job's total estimate amount should be derivable from the sum of labor line items plus parts used.

quotes: Add labor rates and parts to be added into jobs

acceptanceCriteria: Job record contains a laborLineItems list field persisted in stable storage
addLaborLineItem endpoint accepts jobId, laborRateId, optional hours, and description; requires #authorized
removeLaborLineItem endpoint accepts jobId and lineItemId; requires #authorized
listJobs and getJob responses include the laborLineItems array

requirements.text: On the Job Detail page, add a 'Labor' section below the existing fields. Display existing labor line items for the job (labor rate name, type, hours if hourly, and computed amount). Include an 'Add Labor' button that opens an inline form: select a labor rate from listLaborRates, enter hours (if hourly type), and a description. Allow removing individual line items. Show a running subtotal for labor. Use the addLaborLineItem and removeLaborLineItem backend endpoints.

quotes: Add labor rates and parts to be added into jobs

acceptanceCriteria: Labor section is visible on the Job Detail page
Existing labor line items display rate name, type, hours (if hourly), and dollar amount
Add Labor form lets user select a labor rate and enter hours for hourly rates
Removing a line item calls removeLaborLineItem and refreshes the list
A running labor subtotal is displayed below the line items

requirements.text: On the Job Detail page, add a 'Parts Used' section that displays parts already associated with the job via existing usePartOnJob records. Include an 'Add Part' button that opens an inline form: select a part from listParts, enter a quantity to deduct, and confirm. This calls the existing usePartOnJob endpoint. Show part name, SKU, quantity used, and unit cost per line. Display a running parts subtotal.

quotes: Add labor rates and parts to be added into jobs

acceptanceCriteria: Parts Used section is visible on the Job Detail page
Existing parts used are displayed with name, SKU, quantity, and unit cost
Add Part form lets user select a part and enter a quantity
Submitting the form calls usePartOnJob and refreshes the parts list
A running parts subtotal is shown below the line items

requirements.text: Extend the backend to store a signature blob associated with the login/onboarding flow. Add a storeUserSignature(sig: Blob) endpoint restricted to authenticated callers (minimum #authorized) that saves the signature blob keyed by the caller's principal in a stable TrieMap<Principal, Blob>. Add a getUserSignature() query that returns the stored blob for the caller. The signature should be associated with the user profile, not a specific job.

quotes: Integrate the sign on screen option blob storage

acceptanceCriteria: storeUserSignature endpoint accepts a Blob and persists it keyed by caller principal
getUserSignature query returns the stored blob for the caller or null if none
Both endpoints require at minimum #authorized permission
Signature blobs are stored in a stable variable and survive upgrades

requirements.text: On the Login page (LoginPage.tsx) and/or the Profile Setup page (ProfileSetupPage.tsx), add an optional signature capture step after the user authenticates. Display a canvas-based signature pad where the user can draw their signature with a mouse or finger. Include Clear and Save buttons. On Save, convert the canvas drawing to a Blob and call storeUserSignature. Show a confirmation message when saved. Skip the step gracefully if the user dismisses it.

quotes: Integrate the sign on screen option blob storage

acceptanceCriteria: A signature capture canvas is presented to the user after successful login or during profile setup
User can draw a signature using touch or mouse input on the canvas
Clear button resets the canvas
Save button converts the canvas content to a Blob and calls storeUserSignature
A success message is shown after saving
User can skip the signature step without error

requirements.text: Extend the backend Job record to include a photos field: list of Blob (already present as [Blob] per prior spec). Add addJobPhoto(jobId: Nat, photo: Blob) and removeJobPhoto(jobId: Nat, photoIndex: Nat) endpoints restricted to #authorized. Ensure these persist in stable storage and are included in getJob and listJobs responses.

quotes: Blob storage on jobs for pictures(open camera)

acceptanceCriteria: addJobPhoto endpoint appends a Blob to the job's photos array and requires #authorized
removeJobPhoto endpoint removes a photo by index and requires #authorized
Photo blobs persist across canister upgrades in stable storage
getJob response includes the photos array

requirements.text: On the Job Detail page, add a 'Photos' section that displays thumbnail previews of all stored photos for the job. Include a 'Take Photo / Upload' button that uses an HTML file input with accept='image/*' and capture='environment' to open the device camera or file picker. On selection, read the file as an ArrayBuffer, convert to Blob, and call addJobPhoto. Display the uploaded photo as a thumbnail in the section. Include a delete icon on each thumbnail that calls removeJobPhoto.

quotes: Blob storage on jobs for pictures(open camera)

acceptanceCriteria: Photos section is visible on the Job Detail page
Existing job photos are displayed as thumbnail previews
Take Photo / Upload button opens camera on mobile and file picker on desktop
Selected image is uploaded via addJobPhoto and immediately appears as a thumbnail
Delete icon on each thumbnail calls removeJobPhoto and removes it from the display
Loading and error states are handled gracefully

constraints: Owner principal must remain q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe — do not change it
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo as a single actor
Photo and signature blobs must be stored in stable variables to survive canister upgrades
nonGoals: Real-time photo syncing or cloud storage outside the canister
Changes to the calendar or invoice screens
